values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

I hope I didn't glaze over an error. Can you check whether the object files are the same before and after the patch?

Patch Set 2:

If this patch continues to be blocked by patch conflicts, it might make sense to split it up by subdir or small group of related files and don't put them in a series so that some of them can land even if others are blocked.

Patch Set 2:

(4 comments)

Ufff. Very huge patch... cannot complete it at once.

Patch Set 2:

The reason is that I like to see this land before the multi-rail work starts landing. It would be nice to sync this up to upstream since the multi-rail will need to be ported upstream. Otherwise Amir and Olaf are going to have a lot of work ahead of them. The reality is if this doesn't land in the next few weeks this work will be delayed until after the multi-rail work is complete. Normally it takes about 4 to 6 weeks to normally land a patch.

Patch Set 2:

IMO the forensic value of the information provided by 'git blame' outweighs the inconvenience of whitespace issues.

Patch Set 2:

Okay in that case I will abandon this patch.
Abandoned

(14 comments)

The patch is an improvement, though the variable declarations inside functions are gaining tabs when they should really just be a single space.  That is my minor gripe, but I'd rather land this than delay it seeking perfection.  This could also be done in a second patch that fixes all of these cases.

commit.subject: LU-6142 lnet: replace white spaces with tabs for LNet core
commit.message:
LU-6142 lnet: replace white spaces with tabs for LNet core

This work converts all the remaining white spaces left
in the LNet layer to the proper tab format. Fixed all
space prohibited warnings reported by checkpatch. Any
other space issues reported by checkpatch are also
addressed for the code that was retabbed.

Test-Parameters: trivial

Change-Id: I12589439c9532d1d3989deee00aa68c29f84db85
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/19975
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Frank Zago <fzago@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
